Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Condensed tannin/proanthocyanidin branch downstream of anthocyanidin formation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream; branch step immediately downstream of ANS and competing with anthocyanin formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces anthocyanidins to the corresponding 2,3-cis-flavan-3-ols using NADPH or NADH as cofactor, e.g. cyanidin to (-)-epicatechin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms flavan-3-ol building blocks for condensed tannin biosynthesis and diverts flux away from anthocyanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves ruminant nitrogen nutrition and protects against pasture bloat; antioxidant activity with beneficial effects on human cardiac health and immunity; contributes to flavor and astringency in tea and wine through flavan-3-ol components
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat/endothelium in Arabidopsis; young seeds in Medicago truncatula; ectopically accumulated in tobacco petals and Arabidopsis leaves upon transgene expression
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch step/terminal determinant for entry into the condensed tannin branch; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes condensed tannin accumulation and reduces anthocyanin pigmentation when ectopically expressed; ban mutation causes loss of CTs in Arabidopsis seed coat
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtBAN and MtBAN encode anthocyanidin reductase rather than leucoanthocyanidin reductase. 2. Recombinant BAN converted cyanidin, pelargonidin, and delphinidin into the corresponding 2,3-cis-flavan-3-ols, including (-)-epicatechin. 3. Constitutive expression of MtBAN in tobacco petals reduced pink anthocyanin pigmentation and induced CT accumulation. 4. Constitutive expression of AtBAN in Arabidopsis leaves induced CT accumulation in vegetative tissue. 5. BAN functions immediately downstream of ANS in the flavonoid pathway and diverts flux from anthocyanin biosynthesis to CT biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein enzyme assays in E. coli; coupled biochemical assays; HPLC; UV-visible spectrophotometry; HPLC-MS; GC-MS; circular dichroism; 1H NMR; transgenic constitutive overexpression in tobacco and Arabidopsis; DMACA staining; butanol-HCl assay; anthocyanin spectrophotification; mutant phenotype comparison with ban and tt18/ANS mutant
Pathway_Genes.Species: Arabidopsis; tobacco; Medicago truncatula; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana tabacum; Medicago truncatula; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: MtBAN strongly expressed in young developing seeds, weakly in open flowers and flower buds, and very weakly in leaves; BAN in Arabidopsis is expressed in seed endothelium from onset of fertilization to preglobular embryo stage
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Constitutive ectopic expression in transgenic tobacco petals and Arabidopsis leaves; in vitro assays with NADPH or NADH
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Random sequencing of a cDNA library from young developing Medicago truncatula seeds; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful for engineering condensed tannin accumulation in forage crops such as alfalfa to improve protein utilization and reduce pasture bloat; potential use in fruits and vegetables for health-beneficial CTs and in tea/wine for flavor and astringency modification
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ectopic BAN expression reduced anthocyanin pigmentation in tobacco petals, indicating possible tradeoff between CT accumulation and anthocyanin-associated coloration
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper distinguishes core BAN genes from background factors such as TT12 and CT-related transcription factors, which were not the primary experimental subjects in the Results. Minor products were interpreted as epimerization artifacts. BAN activity was observed with both NADPH and NADH; no activity in boiled enzyme or empty-vector E. coli extracts.

Title: Role of Anthocyanidin Reductase, Encoded by BANYULS in Plant Flavonoid Biosynthesis
